message:
test(readme): cover 4 value_counts variants (fd90.229 / br-ulfpm)

Extends readme_advanced_selection_compiles_and_runs to cover the 4
value_counts variants that were previously uncovered:

- Series.value_counts_bins(usize) — binning a numeric Series into
  N quantile-style bins (line 3302)
- DataFrame.value_counts_per_column (line 20168)
- DataFrame.value_counts_map — returns BTreeMap<String, Series>
  (line 20305) — asserts a known column key is present
- DataFrame.value_counts_subset(&[col]) — restrict counts to specific
  columns (line 20363)

Combined with the existing Series.value_counts +
value_counts_with_options coverage, all 6 value_counts API entries
are now integration-tested.

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-uibg","title":"Port Series.dt.microsecond accessor","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:26:21.083034308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T22:35:37.481401788Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T22:35:37.479583594Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-uj2np","title":"fd90.116: index.drop_duplicates(DuplicateKeep) wrong fn (use drop_duplicates_keep) + no Result so ? invalid","description":"fd90.74-115 README audit. Line 1607 in Duplicate Handling shows:

  let unique_idx = index.drop_duplicates(DuplicateKeep::First)?;

Two compounding errors against crates/fp-index/src/lib.rs:
- line 638: pub fn drop_duplicates(&self) -> Self    // NO arg, returns Self (not Result)
- line 646: pub fn drop_duplicates_keep(&self, keep: DuplicateKeep) -> Self

The README passes a DuplicateKeep arg to the no-arg variant (would error E0061), AND uses ? on a value that isn't a Result (would error E0277). The named variant 'drop_duplicates_keep' takes the keep arg but still returns Self, not Result<Self, _>.

A user copy-pasting hits two compile errors at once.

Fix: switch to drop_duplicates_keep(DuplicateKeep::First) and remove the ?. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:59:44.968642390Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:00:55.171835335Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:00:55.169110021Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-ulfpm","title":"fd90.229: Cover 4 value_counts variants (bins/per_column/map/subset)","description":"fd90.229: Cover 4 value_counts variants (bins/per_column/map/subset).

Series.value_counts and DataFrame.value_counts are tested. Missing:
- Series.value_counts_bins(usize) — for binning continuous data (line 3302)
- DataFrame.value_counts_per_column (line 20168)
- DataFrame.value_counts_map (line 20305) — returns BTreeMap<String, Series>
- DataFrame.value_counts_subset(&["col"]) (line 20363)

Scope:
- Extend an existing test with all 4 calls.

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs.
Time estimate: 10-15min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-27T03:33:17.849611283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T03:35:19.206786103Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T03:35:19.204733656Z","close_reason":"Extended readme_advanced_selection_compiles_and_runs to cover the 4 value_counts variants previously uncovered: Series.value_counts_bins(3) (binning a 6-element numeric Series), DataFrame.value_counts_per_column, DataFrame.value_counts_map (asserts BTreeMap contains expected keys), DataFrame.value_counts_subset(&['a']). All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-um5fl","title":"fd90.174: README astype example (id, DType::Utf8) only works if id is Utf8 — to-Utf8 cast unsupported","description":"fd90.74-173 follow-on. The README's Type Coercion example at line 1011 shows:

  let df2 = df.astype_columns(&[("id", DType::Utf8), ("score", DType::Float64)])?;

But cast_scalar_owned at crates/fp-types/src/lib.rs unconditionally rejects DType::Utf8 as a cast target:

  DType::Utf8 => Err(TypeError::InvalidCast { from, to: target }),

So this only works if 'id' is ALREADY Utf8 — not a real cast example. A user with an Int64 'id' column copying this hits InvalidCast.

This contrasts with pandas where df.astype('str') works on any type. FrankenPandas's design choice is to-Utf8 casting goes through to_string-style conversion (Display impl) which isn't currently exposed as a cast target.

Fix: change the README example to a target that actually works for arbitrary-dtype source columns. E.g. use ('id', DType::Float64) or ('count', DType::Int64). Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:09:01.588735162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:10:29.224752628Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T17:10:29.221865649Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-unvl4","title":"fd90.133: JoinError + GroupByError rows claim non-existent join/aggregation-specific variants","description":"fd90.74-132 README audit. Error Architecture rows for JoinError and GroupByError describe non-existent variant categories:

  | JoinError | fp-join | wraps FrameError + join-specific failures |
  | GroupByError | fp-groupby | wraps FrameError + aggregation failures |

Verified actual definitions:

crates/fp-join/src/lib.rs (search 'pub enum JoinError'):
  pub enum JoinError {
      Frame(#[from] FrameError),
      Column(#[from] ColumnError),
  }

crates/fp-groupby/src/lib.rs:91:
  pub enum GroupByError {
      Frame(#[from] FrameError),
      Index(#[from] IndexError),
      Column(#[from] ColumnError),
  }

Both enums are pure transparent-wrapper errors — there are no join-specific or aggregation-specific variants. The README's '+ X failures' phrasing implies bespoke variants that don't exist.

A user pattern-matching expecting Err(JoinError::SomeJoinSpecific(...)) would find no such thing.

Fix: rewrite both rows to honestly describe the wrapper variants:
  | JoinError | fp-join | Frame(FrameError), Column(ColumnError) (transparent wrappers — no join-specific variants today) |
  | GroupByError | fp-groupby | Frame(FrameError), Index(IndexError), Column(ColumnError) (transparent wrappers — no aggregation-specific variants today) |

Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:24:46.027446777Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:25:57.988918219Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T12:25:57.986233957Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
